lecturer_notes: The speaker repeatedly references 'Professor Eagar' in the third person ('Professor Eagar talked about it and will continue to talk about it', 'that's another topic that Professor Eagar taught us in this class', 'Tomorrow, Professor Eagar is going to be lecturing'). Speaker self-references work on the Minneapolis bridge collapse (I-35W) failure analysis and on helicopter blade reverse-engineering IP litigation in aerospace. Likely a forensic/consulting engineer guest lecturer. Editor (RHT) review needed to identify.

proposed_sections.title: Weld inspection standards: ISO vs. AWS
Leak before break and the rubber-sealed pressure vessel
Statistics of demand and capacity; the social criterion
How safety factor erodes with time: the Minneapolis bridge
Helicopter blade reverse-engineering and S-N confidence intervals

cases_referenced.name: I-35W Mississippi River bridge collapse (Minneapolis, 2007)
cases_referenced.frame: Speaker worked on the failure analysis; uses it as exemplar of how locked bearings, construction load, and hot weather combined to erode the as-designed safety factor over 40 years.

cases_referenced.name: Rubber-sealed pressure vessel circumferential crack
cases_referenced.frame: Factor-of-four pressure vessel where a rubber bottom seal bridged a growing circumferential crack, defeating leak-before-break and ending in overpressure failure.

cases_referenced.name: Helicopter rotor blade reverse-engineering (aerospace IP)
cases_referenced.frame: Reverse-engineered helicopter blade with aluminum spar and honeycomb-core composite trailing edge; aluminum alloy substitution required full S-N qualification with confidence intervals.

cases_referenced.name: Honda ATV early production over-design
cases_referenced.frame: Used as exemplar of starting over-designed and optimizing down via service history rather than statistical design from scratch.

cases_referenced.name: Copper pipe self-sealing pitting corrosion
cases_referenced.frame: Hot-water copper pipes with through-thickness pits that self-seal with salts and organics, defeating leak-detection inspection.

cases_referenced.name: Longfellow Bridge (Boston)
cases_referenced.frame: Local exemplar: hundred-year-old bridge carrying the MBTA Red Line; high exposure justifying conservative inspection and repair.
